annotations.label: Algebra over a field

anchor.snippet: an algebra over a field (often simply called an algebra ) is a vector space equipped with a bilinear product

annotations.note: Mathlib's `Algebra R A` class for `CommSemiring R` and `Semiring A` generalizes the field-base notion.

annotations.label: Associative vs non-associative algebra

anchor.snippet: leading to the notions of associative algebras where associativity of multiplication is assumed, and non-associative algebras

annotations.note: Associative algebras are `Algebra` (over `Semiring A`); non-associative variants exist via `NonUnitalNonAssocSemiring`/`NonUnitalAlgHom` but there is no top-level `NonAssocAlgebra` class.

annotations.label: Ring of real square matrices as associative algebra

anchor.snippet: the ring of real square matrices of order n is an example of an associative algebra over the field of real numbers

annotations.note: `Matrix.instAlgebra` makes `Matrix n n α` an `R`-algebra over any base commutative ring, including ℝ.

annotations.label: Euclidean 3-space with cross product as non-associative algebra

anchor.snippet: Three-dimensional Euclidean space with multiplication given by the vector cross product is an example of a nonassociative algebra

annotations.note: `crossProduct` is defined as a bilinear map on `Fin 3 → R` but is not packaged as a (non-associative) algebra instance.

annotations.label: Unital algebra (lead)

anchor.snippet: An algebra is unital or unitary if it has an identity element with respect to the multiplication

annotations.note: The Mathlib `Algebra` class is unital by construction since it extends `Semiring A` (which has `1`).

annotations.label: Unital associative algebra of matrices

anchor.snippet: The ring of real square matrices of order n forms a unital algebra since the identity matrix

annotations.note: `Matrix.instAlgebra` gives `Matrix n n α` as a unital `R`-algebra with identity `1` being the identity matrix.

annotations.label: Algebra over a field (formal definition)

anchor.snippet: Then A is an algebra over K if the following identities hold for all elements

annotations.note: `class Algebra (R A) extends SMul R A` axiomatizes precisely these compatibility identities via `commutes'` and `smul_def'`.

annotations.label: K-algebra homomorphism

anchor.snippet: a homomorphism of K -algebras or K - algebra homomorphism is a K - linear map

annotations.note: `AlgHom R A B` bundles a ring homomorphism that commutes with `algebraMap R _`, equivalent to a multiplicative `R`-linear map.

annotations.label: Unital homomorphism

anchor.snippet: then a homomorphism satisfying f (1 A ) = 1 B is said to be a unital homomorphism

annotations.note: `AlgHom` extends `RingHom`, hence by definition sends `1` to `1`.

annotations.label: K-algebra isomorphism

anchor.snippet: A K -algebra isomorphism is a bijective K -algebra homomorphism

annotations.note: `AlgEquiv R A B` (`A ≃ₐ[R] B`) bundles a bijective `R`-algebra homomorphism.

annotations.label: Subalgebra

anchor.snippet: A subalgebra of an algebra over a field K is a linear subspace that has the property that the product of any two of its elements is again in the subspace

annotations.note: `structure Subalgebra R A` extends `Subsemiring A` with closure under `algebraMap`, matching the closure-under-multiplication-and-scalars notion.

annotations.label: Real line as subalgebra of complex numbers

anchor.snippet: the one-dimensional real line is a subalgebra

annotations.note: The general infrastructure exists (`algebraMap ℝ ℂ`, `Subalgebra.range`/`Algebra.bot`) but the specific `ℝ ⊆ ℂ` subalgebra example isn't a named decl.

annotations.label: Left ideal of an algebra

anchor.snippet: A left ideal of a K -algebra is a linear subspace that has the property that any element of the subspace multiplied on the left

annotations.note: `Ideal R` (a `Submodule R R`) is left-ideal-like for commutative rings; no dedicated `LeftIdeal` of an algebra is defined.

annotations.label: Right ideal and two-sided ideal

anchor.snippet: If (3) were replaced with x · z is in L , then this would define a right ideal

annotations.note: `TwoSidedIdeal R` formalizes the two-sided notion for non-commutative rings; no dedicated `RightIdeal` of an algebra structure.

annotations.label: Every left or right ideal is a subalgebra

anchor.snippet: It follows from condition (3) that every left or right ideal is a subalgebra

annotations.note: No `Ideal.toSubalgebra` or analogous lemma promoting an ideal to a subalgebra was found; also note proper ideals don't contain 1, so this needs a non-unital reading.

annotations.label: Extension of scalars

anchor.snippet: there is a natural way to construct an algebra over F from any algebra over K

annotations.note: Base change `A ⊗[R] B` carries an `S`-algebra structure via `Algebra.TensorProduct.leftAlgebra`, which is the extension-of-scalars construction.

annotations.label: Unital algebra

anchor.snippet: An algebra is unital or unitary if it has a unit or identity element

annotations.note: Same as above: Mathlib's `Algebra` requires `Semiring A`, so all `Algebra` instances are unital.

annotations.label: Zero algebra

anchor.snippet: An algebra is called a zero algebra if uv = 0 for all u , v in the algebra

annotations.note: No named `ZeroAlgebra` construction; one could express it via instances on a module with trivial multiplication but no library decl encodes this.

annotations.label: Unital zero algebra

anchor.snippet: A unital zero algebra is the direct sum K ⊕ V of a field K and a K -vector space V

annotations.note: `Unitization R A` constructs the algebra `R × A` with the appropriate product; specialized to the zero-multiplication `A` it is exactly the unital zero algebra `K ⊕ V`.

annotations.label: Dual numbers as unital zero algebra

anchor.snippet: A classical example of unital zero algebra is the algebra of dual numbers

annotations.note: `DualNumber R` is defined as `TrivSqZeroExt R R` (a special case of `Unitization`), matching the dual-number construction.

annotations.label: Submodules correspond to ideals of unital zero algebra

anchor.snippet: the submodules of a module V correspond exactly to the ideals of K ⊕ V that are contained in V

annotations.note: No correspondence lemma between `Submodule R V` and ideals of `Unitization R V` contained in the second summand was located.

annotations.label: Matrix algebra

anchor.snippet: the algebra of all n -by- n matrices over a field (or commutative ring) K

annotations.note: `Matrix.instAlgebra : Algebra R (Matrix n n α)` is the matrix algebra over any base commutative semiring.

annotations.label: Group algebras

anchor.snippet: group algebras , where a group serves as a basis of the vector space and algebra multiplication extends group multiplication

annotations.note: `MonoidAlgebra R M` is the convolution algebra on functions `M →₀ R`; when `M` is a group this is the usual group algebra.

annotations.label: Polynomial algebra

anchor.snippet: the commutative algebra K [ x ] of all polynomials over K

annotations.note: `Polynomial.algebraOfAlgebra : Algebra R A[X]` gives `R[x]` (= `A[X]`) the polynomial-algebra structure.

annotations.label: Algebras of functions

anchor.snippet: algebras of functions , such as the R -algebra of all real-valued continuous functions defined on the interval

annotations.note: `ContinuousMap.algebra : Algebra R C(α, A)` makes continuous functions into an `R`-algebra under pointwise operations.

annotations.label: Incidence algebras

anchor.snippet: Incidence algebras are built on certain partially ordered sets

annotations.note: `IncidenceAlgebra` is defined in `Combinatorics/Enumerative/IncidenceAlgebra.lean`.

annotations.label: Algebras of linear operators

anchor.snippet: algebras of linear operators , for example on a Hilbert space

annotations.note: `Module.End.instAlgebra : Algebra R (Module.End S M)` provides the algebra of (linear) endomorphisms.

annotations.label: Non-associative algebra

anchor.snippet: A non-associative algebra [ 3 ] (or distributive algebra ) over a field K is a K -vector space A equipped with a K - bilinear map

annotations.note: Mathlib provides `NonUnitalNonAssocSemiring`/`NonUnitalAlgHom` and `Module` with bilinear multiplication, but no top-level `NonAssocAlgebra` class capturing precisely this notion.

annotations.label: Associative K-algebra via ring homomorphism

anchor.snippet: an algebra over a field K is a ring A together with a ring homomorphism

annotations.note: `RingHom.toAlgebra : (R →+* A) → Algebra R A` (with image in the center) realizes this equivalent definition.

annotations.label: Either A is the zero ring or η is injective

anchor.snippet: then one must have either that A is the zero ring , or that η is injective

annotations.note: `FaithfulSMul.algebraMap_injective` gives injectivity under a faithfulness hypothesis; the precise dichotomy (zero ring vs. injective) over a field is not stated as a lemma.

annotations.label: Unital K-algebra homomorphism via rings

anchor.snippet: a unital K -algebra homomorphism f : A → B is a ring homomorphism that commutes with the scalar multiplication

annotations.note: `AlgHom` is defined as a `RingHom` whose `commutes'` field expresses compatibility with `algebraMap`.

annotations.label: Multiplication determined by basis

anchor.snippet: the bilinear multiplication from A × A to A is completely determined by the multiplication of basis elements of A

annotations.note: Bilinearity-from-basis is available via `Basis.constr` / `TensorProduct.lift`, but no dedicated lemma about determining algebra multiplication by its action on basis elements.

annotations.label: Structure coefficients

anchor.snippet: any finite-dimensional algebra can be specified up to isomorphism by giving its dimension

annotations.note: No `structureCoefficients`/`structureConstants` definition for algebras was found.

annotations.label: Study's classification of low-dimensional unital associative algebras

anchor.section: Classification of low-dimensional unital associative algebras over the complex numbers
anchor.snippet: Two-dimensional, three-dimensional and four-dimensional unital associative algebras over the field of complex numbers were completely classified up to isomorphism by Eduard Study

annotations.note: Study's classification of low-dimensional unital associative ℂ-algebras is not present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Two two-dimensional unital associative C-algebras

anchor.section: Classification of low-dimensional unital associative algebras over the complex numbers
anchor.snippet: There exist two such two-dimensional algebras

annotations.note: No statement enumerating two-dimensional unital associative ℂ-algebras up to isomorphism is present.

annotations.label: Five three-dimensional unital associative C-algebras

anchor.section: Classification of low-dimensional unital associative algebras over the complex numbers
anchor.snippet: There exist five such three-dimensional algebras

annotations.note: No enumeration of three-dimensional unital associative ℂ-algebras is present.

annotations.label: Algebra over a ring

anchor.snippet: it is common to consider the more general concept of an algebra over a ring , where a commutative ring R replaces the field K

annotations.note: `Algebra R A` already takes `R : CommSemiring`, directly realizing the ring (and even semiring) base generalization.

annotations.label: Ring is associative algebra over its center

anchor.snippet: A ring A is always an associative algebra over its center , and over the integers

annotations.note: There is an `instance : Algebra (Subring.center R) R` (and `Ring.toIntAlgebra : Algebra ℤ R`) in `Mathlib.Algebra.Algebra.Basic`.

annotations.label: Split-biquaternion algebra

anchor.snippet: the split-biquaternion algebra , which is isomorphic to H × H

annotations.note: No `SplitBiquaternion` definition found; only ordinary `Quaternion` is in Mathlib.

annotations.label: R-algebra structure from ring homomorphism

anchor.snippet: any unital ring homomorphism R → A defines an R -module structure on A , and this is what is known as the R -algebra structure

annotations.note: `RingHom.toAlgebra : (R →+* A) → Algebra R A` (for `φ` landing in the center) gives this construction directly.
